classifications.reason: MIT license for the future public Workshop repository.
Public-facing README examples use demo/template data and link to private workspace setup.
Docs are publishable after client-specific examples, local absolute paths, and private updater host details are reviewed.
Workshop app code is public-source candidate, but bundled resource config and hardcoded private fixtures must be audited.
Tauri resources are limited to classified demo/template client folders. Private client roots must be selected at runtime.
Core Redline code is public-source candidate; legacy private-client audit helpers remain classified until the clean public clone proof removes or privatizes them.
Synthetic fixture client used to prove cross-client behavior.
Fictional Redline demo packet, target, findings, proof gates, and report artifacts for public installs.
Fictional Megaphone demo corpus and post package for public installs.
Empty Redline packet template without real client source material.
Empty Megaphone corpus template without real examples or source material.
Real pilot client packet containing canonical strategy, Notion snapshots, targets, and generated reports. Must not ship in a public repo or bundled app.
Ignored upstream reference clone used for parity review. It may contain mock secrets or fixture data and must not be included in the public source package.
Generated build output must not be committed as source.
Generated Rust/Tauri build output must not be committed as source.
Installed dependencies are ignored for privacy scanning and must not be committed.

blockedTerms.reason: Real pilot client context must be replaced with demo data before public release.
Local absolute paths should not appear in public examples or bundled config.
Connector snapshots are private client source material unless explicitly sanitized.
Generated reports belong in private local workspaces unless they are sanitized demo artifacts.
API keys and tokens must never be committed.
